note: MHClip_ZH is sourced from Bilibili. Annotations follow Davidson et al. (2017) 3-class taxonomy (Hateful/Offensive/Normal), which aligns closely with YouTube's definitions but adds the Offensive category.

rules.name: Discriminatory content
rules.text: Any content that includes discriminatory content or disparages any religion, belief system, race, sexual orientation, or protected group.

rules.name: Attacks and disparagement
rules.text: Attacks, disparagement, or belittlement that target a specific individual or group based on race, religion, gender, age, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, etc.

rules.name: Verbal abuse and personal attacks
rules.text: Verbal abuse or personal attacks against a specific individual or group, antagonizing others and inciting hatred.

rules.name: Hateful ideologies
rules.text: Promoting hateful ideologies via active discussion or display of signs, symbols, flags, slogans representing hateful organizations.

rules.name: Mocking death and disability
rules.text: Content mocking death, sickness, and disability.

rules.name: Disaster jokes
rules.text: Jokes about disasters and tragic social events.

rules.name: Gender-based hate
rules.text: Hateful content directed toward a particular gender group, including misogynistic and misandrist content.

rules.name: Inciting confrontation
rules.text: Content that maliciously provokes wars or conflicts, incites confrontations, and spreads hatred.

exceptions.name: Not explicitly stated
exceptions.description: Bilibili does not publish explicit EDSA-style exceptions. Content moderation is shaped by China's Cyberspace Administration (CAC) regulatory framework, including the 'Nine Prohibitions'.
